other_observers: Oliver Komar, Cole Wild, Tony Leukering, Lary Semo, Paul Lehman, Steve and Joe Mammoser, Joe Himmel, Dick Maxfield, Rachel Hopper; Tony Leukering
specific_location: Weld County Landfill and Black Hollow Reservoir
description: A gull about the size of large California Gull (estimated 22 or 23 inches in length). Head all white with dark eye and smallish mostly black bill with flesh-colored base. Mantle mostly gray but not clean, possibly due to mixture of worn and fresh feathers (this bird would be engaged in its pre-alternate molt of body feathers). Underparts white on the chest, pale gray-brown on the lower chest to belly, with faint brown barring on vent. Wings whitish with faint brown mottling on coverts, and secondaries. Tertials white with faint thin brown bars. Primaries all white. In flight, wings all whitish from below. From above, a pale secondary bar was apparent. Tail heavily marked with thin gray-brown bars, in contrast to a bright white rump, possibly including the base of the rectrices. Pink legs. When sitting, primaries extended moderately far beyond the tail (more than for Herring Gull, less than for Lesser Black-backed Gull).Behaviors: Flying, circling in a thermal, bathing, standing, walking.Call: nonePlumage: immature
